title: AI-Medical PGR Conference
text:
Submit Abstract   




AI-Medical Postgraduate Researcher Conference 2026


The conference is at the intersection of two of the most transformative forces of our time: AI and medicine. It is an event tailored for postgraduate research at the University of Leeds who are pushing boundaries, questioning ideas, and building the tools and insights that will define tomorrow’s clinical landscape. Whether your research explores machine learning for diagnostics, ethics and governance for AI deployment, robotics in surgery, or data-driven public health, this event celebrates the diversity and depth of innovation happening across the field.
AI-Medical 2026 will bring together PhD students, academics, and industry partners, offering a dynamic platform to explore and discuss the latest research.
The conference is being organised by the UKRI Centre for Doctoral Training in AI for Medical Diagnosis and Care.















No matching items






We are inviting the submission of abstracts from postgraduate research students at the University of Leeds.





Clinical Decision Support: AI-powered Screening and Early Detection, Diagnosis, and Treatment and Care
Ethics & Governance: Algorithmic bias, data privacy, explainability (XAI), and regulatory frameworks.
Digital Health: Wearable technology, med-tech, digital twins, and AI-driven patient-centred care.
AI surgical robotics and healthcare scientists
Drug Discovery: AI in pharmacology and large-scale cohort data analysis.





Format: A structured abstract (Background, Methods, Results, and Conclusions). References and images may be included.
Word Limit: Maximum 300 words (excluding any references and image captions).




If your abstract is accepted, you will:

Present Your Work: Deliver a 2-minute spotlight presentation and showcase a research poster during a dedicated session.
Receive Expert Feedback: Engage in a discussion with leading clinicians, AI innovators, and senior academics.
Network & Collaborate: Help forge a multidisciplinary community of PGR students, industry and public sector partners working at the cutting edge of AI in healthcare.















    
        
        Ibrahim Habli
        :University of York
    

    
        
        Jessica Paulsen
        :Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency
